campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary local rule (Ordinance 2326) and moving forward with plans for a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth sports and recreation scholarships
Extension of Ordinance 2326 (temporary local regulation)
Hamilton Avenue bicycle/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 - grant application and feasibility study approval

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could speak about a proposed new rule or law. After hearing public input, the council moved forward with the proposal by skipping the usual first reading process and scheduled it to be formally approved at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community feedback
New ordinance (city rule) being fast-tracked for approval
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to speed up the adoption process

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to approve several routine matters, including completing their 2026 weed control program and establishing new rules for how the council conducts meetings. The council also approved raising two new flags at city facilities (Progress Pride and Juneteenth), discussed affordable housing policies, and heard updates from the regional transit authority about transportation planning.
keyTopics: Completed 2026 weed abatement program
New council meeting rules and procedures adopted
Affordable housing unit transfer exemption policy
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flags approved for city buildings
Regional transit authority (VTA) presentation and housing element planning

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met to review the town's finances from January, check progress on housing and planning goals, and approve a new housing development called Solana Project. The council also updated their approach to health and senior services for the year ahead and made some administrative changes to how meetings are recorded.
keyTopics: January 2026 financial reports showing how the city is spending money
Progress update on adding more housing and updating the General Plan
Approval of Solana Project housing development (Tract 10657)
2026 health and senior services programs and priorities
Administrative updates to council meeting procedures

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to organize upcoming council agendas. The council reviewed what items would be discussed at their April 7 and April 14 meetings, decided which items needed specific time slots, and checked paperwork requirements. They also reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized agenda items for April 7 council meeting
Planned agenda items for April 14 council meeting
Assigned specific time slots to agenda items
Reviewed legal notice requirements for agenda items
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026 where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for public comments. The council also reviewed committee reports and set schedules for upcoming board and commission meetings throughout the city.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Upcoming board and commission meeting schedules
Recreation Division contact for agenda questions (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review and approve applications for federal Community Development Block Grant funding, which helps support affordable housing and community services in Sunnyvale. The meeting also included approval of previous meeting minutes from February.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding applications for housing and community development projects
Review of organizations seeking Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) money
Approval of February 25 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses grow and get the word out about Cupertino. They discussed a program to help businesses that may have missed paying for licenses, ways to encourage shopping locally during summer events, and new incentives to attract and support retail stores and startups in Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses catch up on unpaid licenses
Outreach to shopping centers to boost participation in city programs
Summer of Celebrations campaign to encourage residents to shop locally
Public-private partnerships for sponsoring city events
New incentives and support for retail, small businesses, and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara is holding a meeting on April 2, 2026 where the city's Historical and Landmarks Commission will review and discuss properties or sites of historical importance to the community. Residents can attend in person or join online via Zoom to watch and participate.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submissions accepted by email until noon on meeting day
Zoom meeting details: ID 972 3326 2035 or phone (669) 900-6833

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative housekeeping items like approving previous meeting notes and setting the schedule for future meetings. The council also approved Mayor Montano's travel to a 2026 wellness conference and confirmed a school district representative appointment related to economic development.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and confirmed upcoming meeting schedule
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual Conference
Confirmed new Milpitas Unified School District representative for Economic Development board
Reviewed councilmembers' requested agenda items for future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to honor Women's History Month and approved two main items: a partnership with the American Red Cross to use city buildings as emergency shelters during disasters, and a three-year contract to buy email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster shelter agreement
Email security software purchase ($280,000+ total cost)
Late packet report additions to agenda
